READ: all authenticated users; CREATE: dm_uid==request.auth.uid; UPDATE: isPartyDM; DELETE: isCreator

Campaign resources (images, tokens, maps) shared by DM

firebase_collections.sync_status: ACTIVE - Used by joinParty() code lookup
firebase_collections.vulnerability_note: Fixed: Party codes injection vulnerability patched in firestore.rules v2

nested_subcollection.rls_rules: Nested RLS also defined but not actively used

migration_mapping.supabase.characters.user_id: firebase.characters.{charId}.user_id (mapped from Supabase UID → Firebase UID)
migration_mapping.supabase.parties.name: firebase.parties.{partyId}.name

existing_migration_scripts.purpose: Migrate characters from Supabase to Firebase Firestore
required_env_vars: SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY (Service role key, NOT anon key - bypasses RLS)
FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT (path to service-account.json from Firebase Console)
INCLUDE_DELETED (optional, set to 'true' to migrate soft-deleted chars)

features.image_optimization: Removes oversized base64 imageUrl (>200KB) to fit Firestore 1MB doc limit
features.user_mapping: Maps Supabase UIDs to Firebase UIDs by email (creates users if needed)
features.error_handling: Skips individual chars on error, continues processing

output.dry_run_output: Console log of characters that would migrate (count only)
output.production_output: Firestore documents created/updated, console log with stats

existing_migration_scripts.purpose: Legacy Supabase RPC call, not used in Firebase migration

current_usage: App.tsx imports batchSaveCharacters (but not actively used, Firebase takes priority)
Some components may still reference old saveCharacterToCloud from supabase.ts
supabase_legacy.deprecation_status: DEPRECATED - All new code uses firebase.ts

validated_rules: isPartyMember(partyId) - check uid in members map
vulnerabilities_fixed: FIXED: Party Codes Injection vulnerability (v2 rules)

integration: Line 767: Real-time sync via subscribeToOwnCharacters() - Firebase Firestore onSnapshot

blockers.description: Supabase credentials (VITE_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY) not in active .env
blockers.impact: Cannot run migration script without explicitly setting Supabase env vars

resolution.step_1: Obtain Supabase project URL from Supabase Dashboard
resolution.step_2: Obtain SERVICE ROLE KEY (NOT anon key) from: Supabase → Project Settings → API
resolution.step_4: Run migration script with DRY_RUN=true first to preview

blockers.risk_level: LOW (data not at risk, just blocked until credentials provided)

blockers.description: service-account.json (Firebase Service Account) not present in repo root
blockers.impact: Cannot write to Firestore without service account credentials

resolution.step_1: Go to Firebase Console → Project Settings → Service Accounts

blockers.risk_level: HIGH if credentials exposed; ensure .gitignore includes it

blockers.description: Parties table exists in Supabase but migration script only handles characters
blockers.details: migrate-to-firebase.mjs migrates supabase.characters → firebase.characters, but not supabase.parties
blockers.impact: Party data in Supabase will be orphaned; parties must be recreated in Firebase manually

resolution.option_1: Extend migrate-to-firebase.mjs to handle parties (see RECOMMENDED below)
resolution.option_2: Manually recreate parties in Firebase via app UI

blockers.description: Supabase UIDs do not match Firebase UIDs; migration requires email-based mapping
blockers.details: Supabase uses UUID4; Firebase uses alphanumeric UID. migrate-to-firebase.mjs maps by email.
blockers.impact: If users don't have matching emails in Firebase, their characters will be skipped or auto-created

resolution.step_1: Verify all Supabase users exist in Firebase with SAME EMAIL
resolution.step_2: If not, manually add users to Firebase or use multi-user mode (auto-creates)
resolution.step_3: Review migration logs for 'skipped' or 'auto-created' users
resolution.step_4: Verify user_id field in migrated characters points to correct Firebase UID

blockers.safety: Characters with unmapped users are SKIPPED; not corrupted or lost

blockers.description: Base64 imageUrl fields >200KB cause Firestore doc size > 1MB limit
blockers.details: Firestore has 1MB/document limit; oversized base64 images are stripped during migration
blockers.impact: Characters lose their imageUrl data; can be re-uploaded via app UI

resolution.step_1: Review migration logs for 'Removiendo imageUrl base64 gigante'
resolution.step_2: Export character images separately before migration
resolution.step_3: Re-upload images via app UI after migration
resolution.step_4: Consider using Cloud Storage for large images instead of JSONB

blockers.risk_level: VISUAL ONLY - no data loss, just avatar images

party_migration_gap.description: Supabase 'parties' table not included in migrate-to-firebase.mjs

current_architecture.supabase_status: LEGACY - Connection strings exist in code but not actively used

data_validation.applied_during: Migration script validates each character before writing to Firestore

priority_1_immediate: ✅ Confirm Firebase service-account.json downloaded and secured
⚠️ Run migration script with DRY_RUN=true to preview character count
⚠️ Review logs for any validation errors or skipped records
priority_2_before_production: 🔴 Extend migration script to handle parties (create scripts/migrate-parties-to-firebase.mjs)
🔴 Verify all Supabase users exist in Firebase (by email)
⚠️ Backup Supabase data before running migration (export as JSON)
priority_3_post_migration: 📊 Audit Firestore document counts vs Supabase row counts
⚠️ Test party joins and resource sharing
✅ Decomission Supabase tables after validation period (30 days)
recommendation.estimated_risk: LOW (characters validated, legacy code path exists for fallback)
recommendation.rollback_plan: Keep Supabase online for 30 days; Firestore coexists with localStorage fallback

audit_summary.credentials_available: PARTIALLY (Firebase yes, Supabase requires manual entry)
audit_summary.next_action: Obtain Supabase service role key and run DRY_RUN preview
